case_title: THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. ANNIE HEARNE et al

docket_entries.description: REQUEST FOR DEFAULT REJECTED, PLACED IN PICKUP BOX, AS TO DEFENDANT HEARNE, ANNIE JEROME, ADOLPHUS HEARNE, ANGELA
OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER FILED BY PLAINTIFF THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT ANGELA HEARNE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND BECAUSE THE NOTICE DOES NOT CONFORM TO THE CONTRACTUAL REQUIREMENT THAT A NOTICE OF PROPOSED EVICTION ADVISE THE LESSEE OF AN OPPORTUNITY TO SET FORTH REASONS WHY THE LEASE SHOULD NOT BE TERMINATED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

calendar.matters:
DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-09-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Angela Hearne, 424-1287, defendant and moving party appearing in propria persona

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ANGELA HEARNE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend because the notice does not conform to the contractual requirement that a notice of proposed eviction advise the lessee of an opportunity to set forth reasons why the lease should not be terminated. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (301/PJB/PM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189
